title: The Estate
timelinePosition: Directly follows the Chapter 9 split. Jaina has taken the Barov / Scholomance thread alone while Sylvanas and Cyndia ride south toward Arthas. This chapter ends with Jaina descending; Chapter 12 resumes this thread after a deliberate gap rather than immediate scene-continuation.
openLocation: Eastweald road into the Barov estate, with the deeper source signature pulling beyond the house toward Darrowmere Lake and Caer Darrow.
transport: Riding a Thalassian horse through the Eastweald, then on foot through the estate, then by rowboat across Darrowmere Lake.
immediateObjective: Follow the Barov grain trail into the institution behind the plague and obtain the missing animation-trigger mechanism before institutional delay turns the lead cold.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only.
Keep Sylvanas off-page; her absence should register as missing fieldcraft and missing friction, not as nostalgic longing.
No explicit Mal'Ganis identification or resolution of the old seller / grain-man clue.
No certainty that the Barovs knowingly designed the plague.

povKnowsAtOpen: Chapter 9 split the investigation cleanly: she took the Barov / Scholomance thread while Sylvanas and Cyndia rode south toward Arthas.
Field work from Chapters 5-9 traced contaminated grain contracts back through Barov infrastructure in the Eastweald.
Her six minutes of ritual data from the Cult outpost gave her binding sequences but not the animation trigger; the missing mechanism is the reason she came here alone.
The Chapter 9 read proved the plague is completed through the host body, but not how the dead answer afterward.

Her sensitivity is letting the party identify plague infrastructure earlier than anyone else would, but they are still one step behind the rollout because every shipment they identify has already landed.
Cyndia's approach intelligence plus Jaina's own route work say the Barov surface and the deeper arcane source do not fully match.
The old seller on the north road and the survivor girl's newer grain-man memory remain filed grain-side anomalies, not solved leads.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether the Barovs are architects, collaborators, or respectable fronts for someone deeper.
Whether Scholomance is a live production site or only a research/archive node.
Whether institutional help can reach her before the plague timeline outruns it.
Whether the old seller matters at all.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No certainty that the Barovs knowingly designed the plague.
No resolution of the old seller / grain-man clue.
No rescue assumption; Sylvanas and Cyndia are on the parallel Arthas thread, not on a same-day escort line.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: The Barov estate and Scholomance are confirmed as live plague-support infrastructure.
Jandice's vanity is the access vector into the deeper levels.
Antonidas and the Kirin Tor cannot move at the speed this crisis requires.
Jaina descends alone carrying the research log and the unresolved mechanism question.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Jaina sees clearly enough and moves fast enough, she can carry this lead alone.
pov.surfaceObjective: Reach the missing mechanism before the Barov trail gets buried by politics, delay, or a cleaned site.
pov.pressureSource: She is walking into a live institution without Sylvanas, without Cyndia, and without the tactical voice that usually prices the room before Jaina starts following the theory.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina leans harder on rank, analysis, and institutional identity because those are the tools she still has in hand.
pov.chapterTest: The Barov path only opens if Jaina uses authority and intellect inside rooms she is not actually safe enough to read alone.
pov.forcedChoice: Pretend she can replace missing fieldcraft with speed and brilliance, or accept that direct, exposed entry is safer than trying to imitate a ranger she is not.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina gets farther in, but the chapter makes plain that competence is not the same thing as safe solo judgment.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 12 opens with Jaina deeper inside the answer and more exposed than she would have let herself admit two chapters ago.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Missing the woman who would ask how long this takes, check the exits, and make Jaina price the room before she chases the theory.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Jaina knows Sylvanas matters, but she is still not fully pricing how much of her own safety has been coming from Sylvanas's judgment.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Every escalation inside Barov ground proves that being smart and being safe are not the same thing.
throughPov.interactionRule: Keep Sylvanas off-page. She should appear as missing questions, missing habits, and missing friction, not as nostalgic commentary.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Split by necessity, both threads understrength.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership is proven by removal. Every escalation now lands without the missing half there to check it.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Let the absence hit the work first and the feeling second.
team.doNotOverstateRule: No pining. The point is exposed judgment.

entries.rule: Off-page on the southbound Arthas thread with Cyndia. Do not imply same-scene support.
Off-page with Sylvanas; her earlier estate intelligence feeds Jaina's approach, but she is not physically present.
Scholarly gatekeeper and vanity vector into Scholomance.
Political surface for the estate and grain operations.
Off-page pressure until the final descent; his presence is felt before it is seen.
Live institutional background, not shambling dungeon dressing.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 11 runs in parallel on Sylvanas and Cyndia's Arthas track. Chapter 12 returns to Jaina after a deliberate gap, already inside Kel'Thuzad's inner sanctum.

factsLockedForChapter11: Jaina is inside Scholomance alone and below the curated tour route.
The Barov estate and Scholomance are confirmed as live plague-support infrastructure.
Jandice's vanity is the access vector into the deeper levels.
Antonidas and the Kirin Tor cannot move at the speed this crisis requires.
factsLockedForChapter12: Jaina descends carrying the research log and the unresolved Chapter 9 mechanism question.
Chapter 12 resumes below after a deliberate gap with the conversation already in progress.
The institution Jaina entered uses Kirin Tor-derived methods and notation, which sharpens the mirror with Kel'Thuzad before he appears.
factsNotYetEarned: Kel'Thuzad's direct disclosures about the Lich King and the Burning Legion.
Sylvanas's arrival or any rescue / recovery beat.
Any repair of the split partnership.

scenes.title: The Absence
scenes.sceneFunction: Show how bad the split is in practice, then upgrade the Barov trail from paperwork suspicion to site-scale alarm.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The Barov name sits at the center of the grain contracts; Jaina has incomplete ritual data and no escort.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina begins the chapter still believing speed and clarity can replace the tactical partner she left behind; the estate has to prove otherwise.

enrichment.detail: Canon ties House Barov to broad Lordaeron landholdings and to Caer Darrow on Darrowmere Lake, so a maintained Eastweald approach of productive orchards, wagon-rutted roads, and old-money upkeep is the right visual before the deeper necromantic pull tips the scene from prosperous estate country into institutional wrongness.

beats.emotion: The absence is operational before it is personal.
beats.dilemma: Arrive openly under Kirin Tor authority, or attempt fieldcraft she does not actually possess and lose time she already cannot afford.
beats.decision: Use institutional identity and direct approach. Better to force the house to react than to pretend she can stalk it like a ranger.
beats.valueShift: alone and exposed -> purposeful but announced
newOnPageInformation: Jaina now thinks in terms of timing and cost, but has nobody to answer the question of how long.
The split has already altered her habits even before she names the reason.
Open approach will tell whoever controls the estate exactly who is coming.
Jaina misses Sylvanas most as the person who would price exits, exposure, and the room's real danger before the room closes.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether open authority buys access or only gives the enemy time to stage-manage.
sensoryAnchors: Thalassian horse sweating under cold Eastweald air
wagon ruts cut deep into an old-money road
orchards that read prosperous until the wrongness catches in the leaves
beats.transitionIntent: discovery-hook: the estate comes into view and forces the abstract Barov clue into physical reality
beats.plantedThread: Sylvanas matters most here as the missing person who would make Jaina price the room correctly.
beats.verifierNotes: Immediate post-chapter9 carry: Jaina is working from Cyndia's estate notes and incomplete ritual data, and this open approach should read as refusal to fake Sylvanas's fieldcraft after Lyriel's death.

beats.goal: Assess the Barov estate and identify the source of concentrated necromantic contamination.
beats.conflict: The estate is beautiful, functioning, and visibly prosperous; the arcane pressure says something beneath or beyond it is a facility.

outcome.summary: Jaina confirms she has reached the right place, and the scale is worse than she expected.

newOnPageInformation: The Barov lands are still operating as a live agricultural hub with regular wagon traffic.
The strongest signature radiates past the house toward Darrowmere and Caer Darrow, not from the manor surface alone.
The ward architecture reads like infrastructure built over time, not an improvised plague depot.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much the household surface knows about the depths it sits above.
sensoryAnchors: Barov crest over the gate
workers moving like employees, not prisoners
tingle in Jaina's hands like standing beside an active ward vault
beats.transitionIntent: pressure-test: Jaina puts Kirin Tor authority against the polished surface and watches how the house answers

scenes.title: The Barovs
scenes.sceneFunction: Let the Barovs turn suspicion into managed access and install Jandice as both guide and trap.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The estate is a mask for something deeper; Jaina needs entry without losing the initiative entirely.
scenes.arcPressure: Every layer of managed courtesy tempts Jaina to keep pressing as a Kirin Tor authority even while the lake crossing prices what she loses by entering on their terms.

enrichment.detail: Alexei should read as old-money Lordaeron wealth: temperature-regulating enchantments, expensive imported comforts, and practiced civility covering deliberate detachment. Jandice should read as the family's real scholarly competence center — the peer-speed mind Alexei hands Jaina to the moment the problem becomes intellectual.

beats.goal: Gain access to the estate's grain operations and the scholarly activity at Caer Darrow.
beats.conflict: Alexei Barov is courteous, unsurprised, and politically prepared; he never gives Jaina the confusion or panic an honest household should show.

outcome.summary: Jaina gets access, but only on a curated route the Barovs control.

newOnPageInformation: Alexei expected the possibility of scrutiny and has a polished victim-script ready.
He frames Barov grain losses as proof the family should welcome investigation.
Jandice, not an overseer or steward, is the person chosen to handle Jaina.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much Alexei knows about the lower levels specifically.
Whether curated access can be leveraged into real access.
sensoryAnchors: excellent tea in a room too controlled to feel rural
unlit hearth with enchanted warmth instead of flame
Alexei's still hands resting on the chair arms as if he has already survived this conversation
beats.transitionIntent: new-guide: Jandice enters and turns a political problem into an intellectual one
beats.verifierNotes: Keep Alexei's polish short of final guilt proof. Continuity still allows knowing collaboration, partial knowledge, or heavily controlled presentation; do not collapse the distinction yet.

beats.goal: Assess the guide the Barovs have assigned and find the weakness in her control of the tour.
beats.conflict: Jandice is a genuine intellectual peer, not decorative nobility; curiosity and threat become the same thing the moment she starts citing Jaina's work.

outcome.summary: Jaina identifies pride as leverage, but the guide is far more capable than expected.

newOnPageInformation: Jandice has Kirin Tor-adjacent training and the hand-marks of a real laboratory practitioner.
She knows Jaina's published work closely enough to engage it at full register.
Alexei visibly relaxes once Jandice takes charge, confirming where the real competence sits.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jandice's need for validation outruns her discipline.
How much freedom she actually has under the authority she calls the master.
sensoryAnchors: reagent scarring on Jandice's hands
scholarly robes cut like Dalaran dress in Barov colors
Jandice's gaze measuring Jaina the way Jaina measures her back
beats.transitionIntent: threshold-crossing: Jandice leads Jaina to the lake crossing, where access becomes physical commitment
beats.dialogueGuidance: Let Jandice start in curated host mode, then slip into faster technical register the moment her pride is engaged.

beats.emotion: Professional caution sharpened by the knowledge that this is the last easy exit.
beats.dilemma: Report out now and leave with incomplete data, or cross the lake because the precise answer she needs is obviously ahead.
beats.decision: Board the boat and accept the curated route.
newOnPageInformation: Darrowmere's outer ward line doubles as a monitoring boundary.
Easy extraction ends the moment Jaina leaves the shore.
Jandice rows like someone who has made this crossing hundreds of times.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina will be treated as guest, peer, or contained witness on the island.
sensoryAnchors: green-black water that absorbs light instead of reflecting it
mist closing behind the boat
brief shimmer when the boat passes the outer ward
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-hook: the island fortress resolves into Scholomance's public face

scenes.title: Scholomance
scenes.sceneFunction: Show Scholomance as a real institution and let the mirror start before Kel'Thuzad ever appears.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Caer Darrow is live, warded, and intentionally presented; Jandice may be pried open through vanity.
scenes.arcPressure: The institution is legible because it shares Jaina's training DNA; the more clearly she reads it, the harder it becomes to keep intelligence hunger separate from danger.

enrichment.detail: Caer Darrow is a lake-island fortress whose crypts were repurposed into Scholomance's necromantic classrooms and laboratories. The atmosphere should stay institutional rather than ruin-gothic first: lecture halls, modified Dalaran-style ward logic, research traffic, and staff routines before the deeper horror takes over.

beats.goal: Map the scope and methodology of the institution Jandice is willing to show.
beats.conflict: The tour is curated, but even the safe face of Scholomance is rigorous enough to prove the operation is far beyond rumor or rogue experimentation.

outcome.summary: Jaina confirms real scale, but every answer makes the institution larger and more disciplined than feared.

beats.valueShift: threshold crossed -> academy facade hiding a real engine
newOnPageInformation: Scholomance functions as a live academic institution rather than a dead ruin or isolated lair.
Dalaran ward protocols have been modified rather than discarded, meaning the work grows out of orthodox training.
Staff choreography shows the visit was anticipated from the moment Jaina crossed the outer ward.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where the deepest production levels are.
Whether the master is observing directly or only through the building.
sensoryAnchors: lecture hall with necromantic diagrams on a schoolroom board
preserved hand left on an instructor's desk as casually as a pointer
researchers moving with the concentration of any serious academy
beats.transitionIntent: mirror-trigger: the research wing puts Jaina directly in front of the thought-pattern she cannot dismiss

beats.dilemma: Use the research logs to fill the Chapter 9 gap, or step back from a mind that thinks like hers.
beats.decision: Read. The intelligence value is too high, and the ease of comprehension is part of the warning.
beats.valueShift: academy facade hiding a real engine -> mirror absorbed
newOnPageInformation: Antonidas's notation system governs Kel'Thuzad's logs in details Jaina uses herself.
The logs carry the same problem forward and show where the missing animation-trigger answer must live, but not the complete waking sequence.
The logs imply preservation comes in degrees rather than one flat undead state.
Jandice wanted the Kirin Tor prodigy to see the quality of the work, not merely its existence.
beats.disclosureProvenance: The research-log mirror comes from the shared Kirin Tor notation lineage described in stories/the-plague-road/plans/kirin-tor-thread.md; the mechanism gap aligns with stories/the-plague-road/plans/plague-mechanism.md.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether reading these files earns deeper access or simply marks Jaina as recruitable.
sensoryAnchors: cracked leather log warm from recent use
familiar margin symbol striking harder than any title page
ink-and-reagent smell that feels too much like Dalaran
beats.transitionIntent: deeper-read: now that Jaina recognizes the thought-pattern, she reads the lower wards more aggressively and notices what the tour omits
beats.plantedThread: Kel'Thuzad is already inside the chapter before he appears because his method is written into the room.
beats.verifierNotes: Antonidas-lineage notation is a continuity callback, but the complete animation trigger is still missing here; treat this as partial theoretical alignment, not solved mechanism.
beats.verifierModification: Reduced the mechanism disclosure from near-solution to partial theoretical alignment so Chapter 10 remains the entry-and-choice chapter and Chapter 12 still owns the full explanation.

beats.goal: Locate the true production levels and identify the authority chain below Jandice.
beats.conflict: Ward density outstrips the visible rooms, and Jandice's first controlled crack reveals that someone below outranks her completely.

outcome.summary: Jaina maps the depths and confirms a hidden core, but reaching it will mean surrendering initiative.

newOnPageInformation: Lower levels hold active containment, enhancement, and production infrastructure.
Jandice's authority stops above private research areas she will not name directly.
Jaina's arcane signature has almost certainly been tracked since she crossed the lake.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether outside support can still be summoned in time.
Whether going lower is entry or entrapment.
sensoryAnchors: stone floor humming like a struck tuning fork
teeth-ache pressure from ward density below
the title the master used like an ordinary departmental rank
beats.transitionIntent: institution-test: Jaina now has to test the institution behind her against the urgency of the one below

sceneLevelWarnings: All three beats in this scene darken in the same direction. The prose will need crisp differentiation between institutional scale, methodological mirror, and hidden-core discovery so the middle does not blur.

scenes.title: The Decision
scenes.sceneFunction: Force institutional failure, then let Jandice's vanity open the final door into the controlled descent.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Scholomance is real, the lower levels are active, and someone below is already aware of Jaina.
scenes.arcPressure: With Antonidas too slow and Jandice offering the missing mechanism, Jaina has to choose between safe partial proof and the dangerous gratification of understanding more.

enrichment.detail: Scholomance should feel like an anti-Dalaran built from Dalaran's own bones: sending-stone use strained by ward pressure, passive surveillance worked into the architecture, and theory discussions framed in the same disciplined vocabulary Antonidas taught Jaina. The uncanny part is not chaos but institutional polish bent toward atrocity.

beats.dilemma: Obey Antonidas and extract with partial proof, or keep pressing while the missing field impressions from Chapter 9 are still fresh enough to matter.
beats.decision: Tell Antonidas only that she will document everything and leave safely. She does not disclose that she intends to go deeper.
beats.valueShift: hidden master confirmed -> institution fails again
newOnPageInformation: The Kirin Tor cannot move at plague speed without political clearance it will not get in time.
Jaina's sensory memory from the Cult outpost is degrading even though the written notes are not.
The private room Jandice leaves her in is listening through passive wards.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the sending-stone call was intercepted.
Whether Antonidas would have forbidden the descent if she had told the whole truth.
sensoryAnchors: waterlogged distortion in Antonidas's voice
weak blue sending-stone glow fighting heavy ward pressure
window over Darrowmere making the outside feel impossibly far away
beats.transitionIntent: last-lever: with institutional backing denied, Jaina turns fully to the one pressure point still available — Jandice's need to be understood
beats.verifierNotes: Institutional help is still behind field tempo. Do not imply Antonidas can mount same-day extraction or rescue from this call.

beats.goal: Engage Jandice on the theoretical gap in the plague mechanism and win a path to the deeper levels.
beats.conflict: Jaina must play interested colleague rather than investigator, feeding Jandice's pride without losing sight of what that role costs.

outcome.summary: Jandice opens the theoretical door, but only because Jaina commits to the one tactic she still owns: meeting her as a peer instead of pretending she can leave the room untouched.

beats.valueShift: institution fails again -> the Lie provides an opening
newOnPageInformation: Jandice finds the animation-trigger problem elegant and almost overexplains it.
Resonance surfaces as the key concept organizing the missing trigger problem rather than solving it outright.
Resonance suggests conditioned tissue answers through patterned amplification, not simple external command.
Kel'Thuzad is already aware of Jaina and willing to consider letting her lower.
beats.disclosureProvenance: The mechanism bridge expands the host-body model already established in stories/the-plague-road/plans/plague-mechanism.md; the emotional leverage comes from Jandice's validation hunger in stories/the-plague-road/characters/jandice.md.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Kel'Thuzad wants from the meeting he is permitting.
Whether Jandice understands how much ground she is giving away.
sensoryAnchors: Jandice's hands sketching theory faster than her mouth can contain it
pleasure in her expression when she reaches the interesting part
the word resonance landing harder than any accusation could
beats.transitionIntent: controlled-access: Jandice leaves to consult the master, and the answer that comes back is permission rather than refusal
beats.dialogueGuidance: Jaina should bait recognition, not morality: let the conversation hinge on rigor, elegance, and the part of the theorem Dalaran would have stopped short of naming.
beats.verifierModification: Reframed resonance as the key concept and access lever, not the complete solution, so Jaina still has a real reason to descend and Chapter 12 still earns the full model.

beats.goal: Reach the lower levels where the complete mechanism lives.
beats.conflict: Access is granted instead of seized, proving Jaina is already walking a path someone else wants her to walk.

outcome.summary: Jaina reaches the lower route, but only because Kel'Thuzad allows it.

beats.valueShift: the Lie provides an opening -> committed descent
newOnPageInformation: The lower staircase is older crypt architecture repurposed for live research traffic.
Wards thicken and the luminescent fungi answer Jaina's magical presence as she passes.
Jaina is still carrying the research log without realizing she never put it down.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How long the descent and the meeting below will last.
Whether she can leave with the data if the conversation goes wrong.
sensoryAnchors: old stone worn smooth by centuries of feet
cold dry air carrying preservative compounds
door closing behind her without locking and still feeling like a trap
beats.transitionIntent: parallel-gap: Chapter 11 covers the Arthas thread elsewhere; Chapter 12 resumes below after a deliberate gap with the conversation already in progress
beats.verifierNotes: This handoff opens a deliberate Chapter 11/12 gap. Do not replay the staircase or treat Chapter 12 as same-moment scene continuation.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beat 10 is the chapter's only real upward pivot. The prose will need to make Jaina's turn toward intellectual gambit read as agency, not convenience.

warnings: Beats 2-9 form an extended darkening run — estate alarm, managed access, threshold crossing, institutional scale, handwriting mirror, hidden master, institutional failure. Beat 10 is the only real upward pivot, so Jaina's shift from authority reliance to intellectual gambit must feel like a genuine psychological turn rather than a plot convenience.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter9's ridge split: Jaina rides east with Cyndia's estate-approach notes, Barov route scraps, and partial ritual data bought with Sylvanas's blood and Lyriel's death. The opening deliberately trades Eastweald trap-country for polished estate surface, but Jaina's choice of open authority over improvised stealth grows from that fresh correction: she no longer trusts herself to fake the fieldcraft she just watched fail at lethal cost.

craftResearch.findings: Reveal enough to sharpen curiosity, but keep the decisive answer just out of reach. Applied here by reducing the mechanism disclosure so Chapter 10 promises the answer without stealing Chapter 12's payoff.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: suspense craft articles

craftResearch.findings: Threat should rise in steps: access, commitment, institutional wrongness, surveillance, failed outside help, then trap. Chapter 10 already had the staircase; the verification kept each step distinct so the danger does not blur.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: suspense craft articles

craftResearch.topic: pacing balance between pressure and breath
craftResearch.findings: Investigative suspense needs brief reflective beats between escalations. Sequel beats 1, 5, 7, and 9 were treated as load-bearing breathing room inside a long darkening run.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: suspense craft articles

craftResearch.findings: A dangerous institution lands best when normal professional surfaces hide the threat. The estate, lake crossing, and academy facade were evaluated as curated civility over live atrocity, not generic gothic ruin.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: suspense craft articles

craftResearch.topic: chapter endings built on unresolved descent
craftResearch.findings: A strong cliff edge closes the immediate beat while widening the next danger. Beat 11 works because access is granted instead of seized, leaving Jaina committed on someone else's terms.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: suspense craft articles

pacing.notes: The 11-beat chapter rises cleanly from announced entry to committed descent. Sequel beats 1, 5, 7, and 9 provide breathing space after pressure jumps at 2, 4, 6, and 8. Beats 2-9 form one long darkening run, so beat 10 was calibrated to feel like a genuine tactical pivot rather than one more downward slide. Opening lands in motion and choice, not recap. Closing leaves Jaina descending on someone else's permission.

structure.notes: The chapter stays in its proper lane: entry, curated tour, choice. Mid-chapter turn lands at beat 7 when the handwriting mirror personalizes the institution. Arc-position promises are earned: missing fieldcraft pressures the opening, managed courtesy tests her use of authority, the forced choice resolves into exposed intellectual entry, and the close proves competence is not the same as safe solo judgment. Two disclosures were reduced so Chapter 12 still owns the full ideological duel and complete trigger explanation.

character.notes: POV stays cleanly inside Jaina's perception. She drives every scene through decisions: open approach, accepted crossing, reading the logs, partial lie to Antonidas, and baiting Jandice deeper. Alexei, Jandice, Antonidas, and the off-page Kel'Thuzad serve distinct functions rather than overlapping. Beat 10 was sharpened so Jaina's intellectual gambit reads as agency under pressure, not convenience. Sylvanas remains off-page as missing fieldcraft rather than displaced romance.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent entries were verified. Weakest carries rechecked: beat 2 to 3 lands as social pressure becoming controlled access, beat 8 to 9 lands as hidden-master alarm forcing the Antonidas test, and beat 10 to 11 lands as permission turning into trap. No missing transitionIntent fields needed addition. One intentional break remains at beat 11 for the Chapter 11/12 parallel gap. Chapter bridge added to lock chapter 9's split to chapter 10's opening choice.

continuity.notes: 4 beats are new and 7 beats are evolutions; callback density is 0%, appropriate for a forward-driving entry chapter. No memory contradiction on names, geography, or institutional lag. Fixed two overclaims against live threads: the mechanism is partial again, and Barov presentation stays short of final guilt proof. Required memory now points the writer at the relationship scar, Barov-route thread, Antonidas lag, command refusal, Kel'Thuzad vector, and partial mechanism file.

modifications.description: Added a direct bridge from chapter 9 so Jaina's opening choice carries Cyndia's estate notes, the Barov packet, and the blood-cost lesson about not faking fieldcraft.
Reduced beat 7 from near-answer to partial theoretical alignment, preserving Chapter 12 as the scene that actually delivers the complete animation trigger.
Reframed beat 10 so resonance becomes the key concept and access lever rather than the whole solution, giving Jaina a stronger reason to descend.
Added verifier notes preserving institutional lag and Barov-culpability ambiguity so the chapter advances the lead without overstating final guilt or available rescue.
